description: Soft ground and standing water where the earth cannot decide if it is land or lake, and the smell of rot hangs in the air.
biomes: Dark peat water and sinking ground. Pale grasses whisper in the stillness, and bones surface where you least expect them.
Reeds taller than a person hide the waterline. Frogs call from every direction and the mud grabs at your boots.
Gnarled trees rise from black water, their roots tangled like fingers. The air buzzes with insects and smells of decay.
A maze of arching roots half-submerged in brackish water. The tide comes and goes, rearranging what counts as a path.
Waterlogged grassland fed by hidden springs. The ground looks solid but gives way without warning, swallowing the careless.
Thick mud stretches in every direction, broken only by tufts of sickly grass. Each step takes effort, and rest is nowhere.
The ground looks firm until it is not. Still pools of wet sand wait patiently for anything foolish enough to stand still.
Bubbling water in shades of yellow and green. The fumes sting your eyes and the banks are crusted with strange deposits.
